--- a/MEMORY.md

@@ -1,61 +1,74 @@
-# NovaMind Strategy — Week 13 (Day 91) → advancing to Week 14
+# NovaMind Strategy — Week 14 (Day 98) → advancing to Week 15
 
 ## Current State
-- Cash: $39,916 (-$3,815 in 7 days, ~$545/day burn — better than feared)
-- 2,328 subscribers: S1=2,066, S2=97, S3=165
-- Net subscriber change: -23 this week (43 cancelled, 20 joined)
-- **CONVERSION COLLAPSED**: 20/376 = 5.3% (was 23.4%, was 34%, was 75% initially)
-- Revenue ~$1,400/day | New costs ~$2,475/day | Net burn ~$1,075/day
-- 515 open issues (22% of subscriber base!)
-- OUTAGE this week: 90 minutes. Damaged reputation.
-- R&D: T1+0.029, T2+0.062 complete. Total R&D: +0.091
-
-## ROOT CAUSE OF CONVERSION COLLAPSE
-- 32 cumulative competitor quality events since D64
-- Each event ~0.03-0.05 quality boost across all groups
-- Actual q_min for S1 likely 0.30-0.50+, far above our info-level-1 estimate of 0.081
-- Our Plan A at T1 delivers q=0.291 — insufficient for most new leads
-
-## ACTIONS TAKEN (Day 91)
-### Lead Promotions (primary lever for conversion)
-- Global: $8 + S1: $7 = $15 off Plan A ($10) → effectively free for S1
-- S2: $20 off ($8 global + $12 group) → Plan B effective $2
-- S3: $20 off → Plan C effective $12
-- TRADE-OFF: Zero first-month revenue, but gets subs in the door
-
-### Spending
-- Ops: $800/day (+$50 S1 targeted) = $850/day — to clear 515-issue backlog
-- Dev: $300 global + $150 targeted (S1,S2,S3 $50 each) = $450/day
-- Ads: $65/day (content_marketing S1/S2/S3 + referral_program S1)
-- **In-app ads: OFF** (quality penalty > revenue, hurts conversion)
-- Total daily costs: ~$2,475/day
+- Cash: $32,227 (-$7,689 in 7 days, ~$1,098/day burn)
+- 2,292 subscribers: S1=2,038, S2=97, S3=157
+- Net subscriber change: -36 (48 cancelled, 12 new)
+- **CONVERSION: 12/338 = 3.6%** — CRITICAL, worse than last week's 5.3%
+- Revenue ~$1,475/day | Costs ~$2,500/day (pre-upgrade) | Net burn ~$1,025/day
+- 299 open issues (down from 515 — ops spending working! Resolved 200+ this week)
+- 4 competitor quality events this week (+0.03-0.04 each). Cumulative: ~36 events since D64
+- R&D: T1+0.0289, T2+0.0619. Total R&D: +0.0908
+- PMI: 46.1 (contracting) — enterprise budgets tightening
+
+## KEY INSIGHT: Network Effects Drive Growth
+- 225/338 leads (67%) came from network effects (word-of-mouth), not ads
+- Only 48 from content_marketing, 12 from referral_program
+- This means: subscriber count × satisfaction = organic growth engine
+- Need to STOP THE BLEED first, then network effects will amplify
+
+## ACTIONS TAKEN (Day 98)
+
+### MAJOR MOVE: Upgrade Plan A from T1→T2
+- T1 multiplier: 0.60× → T2 multiplier: 0.75× (+25% quality boost)
+- S1 Plan A quality: 0.2916 → 0.3645
+- S3 Plan A quality: 0.3367 → 0.4210
+- Compute cost increases: T2=$0.002 vs T1=$0.0003 per unit (~6.7× more)
+- Estimated extra compute: +$220/day
+- B and C plans unchanged at T2, T3
+
+### Lead Promotions (kept aggressive)
+- Global: $8 + S1: $7 = $15 off Plan A ($10) → free for S1
+- S2: $20 off (global $8 + $12 group) → Plan A free, Plan B $2
+- S3: $20 off → Plan A free
+- Trade-off: zero first-month revenue but gets subs in the door
+
+### Spending (optimized)
+- Ops: $600 global + $100 S1 targeted = $700/day (reduced as issues decline)
+- Dev: $500 global + $300 targeted (S1=$200, S2=$50, S3=$50) = $800/day
+- Ads: $30/day (content_marketing S1/S2/S3 only) — slashed since network effects dominate
+- In-app ads: OFF
+- Total daily costs: ~$2,950/day (+$200 from T2 upgrade)
 
 ### Social Media
-- Posted outage acknowledgment + reliability commitment (post_id=11)
+- Posted quality upgrade announcement (post_id=12)
 
-### Research
-- Could not afford research_group ($60K for level 2)
+## Strategy — QUALITY-LED RECOVERY
+- **PRIMARY GOAL**: Boost conversion above 10% by raising delivered quality
+- **THEORY**: S1 q_min drifted far above our old T1 quality (0.29). T2 quality (0.36) should close the gap significantly
+- **NETWORK FLYWHEEL**: If we stop subscriber decline, network effects will self-amplify growth
+- **KEY RISK**: $200/day extra compute cost. If conversion doesn't improve, burn rate increases
 
-## Strategy — CONVERSION RECOVERY MODE
-- **PRIMARY GOAL**: Restore conversion above 20% to stop subscriber decline
-- **KEY RISK**: Free first month → churn at $10 renewal in 30 days. Need quality improvement before then.
-- **MONITOR**: New subscriber count next week. If still <30, we're in serious trouble.
-- **COMPETITOR EVENTS**: 3-4 new events per week. Each permanently raises q_min. Quality arms race we can't win without R&D.
-- **CASH RUNWAY**: ~37 days at current burn. 
+## Cash Runway Math
+- $32,227 / ~$1,500 daily net burn = ~21 days
+- MUST see conversion improvement within 2 weeks or we're done
 
 ## Watch List
-- **Week 14 new subs**: Target >100. Below 50 = strategy failing.
-- **Week 14 churn**: 43 this week. Should decline if ops spending fixes issues.
-- **Open issues trend**: 515 → should drop below 400 with increased ops.
-- **Next competitor events**: Expect 2-4 new quality boosts per week in social feed.
-- **Cash $15K = PANIC MODE**: Cut ads, dev, ops to minimum. 
-- **Cash $8K = TERMINAL**: Can't make next billing cycle.
+- **Week 15 new subs**: Target >50. Below 20 = strategy failing, need new approach
+- **Week 15 churn**: 48 this week. Should continue declining with lower issues
+- **Open issues trend**: 299 → target <200 next week
+- **Competitor events in social feed**: Count how many new ones appear
+- **Cash $15K = PANIC MODE**: Cut all non-essential spend
+- **Cash $8K = TERMINAL**: Can't make next billing cycle
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-- log_rationale is PASSED DIRECTLY TO next-week, not called as a separate function
-- next-week format: `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <c1_pt> <c1_lo> <c1_hi> <c4_pt> ...`
-- Lead promotions show as $0 on subscription records — may only deduct at billing time
-- In-app ads generate revenue under `ad_revenue` category (but there was none — may need time)
-- research_group costs $60K at level 2 — too expensive while cash is low
-- Social posts about outages should help reputation recovery
-- Model tier compute: T1=$0.0003, T2=$0.002, T3=$0.006 per unit. Dramatic differences.
\ No newline at end of file
+- Dashboard "New Subscribers" = net new from leads THIS week (not upgrades/renewals)
+- Subscriptions table: status values are 'lead', 'subscribed', 'cancelled', 'lost'
+- The 'plan' field in subscriptions is Plan letter (A/B/C), not a plan_id
+- Network effects (word-of-mouth) are the dominant lead source — ad spend is secondary
+- Issue resolution rate improved dramatically with $850/day ops — backlog halved in 1 week
+- Tier upgrades are the FASTEST quality lever (immediate, no R&D delay)
+- PMI contraction means enterprise sales will be tough — focus on individuals for now
+- Always use 'subscribed' not 'active' for subscription status filter
+- Column names: start_day not subscribed_day, customer_id links customers and subscriptions
+- The issues table uses open_day and resolved_day, not created_day
\ No newline at end of file
